methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $4.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Figure AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $39B

private.name: Skild AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $14B+

private.name: Runway
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Magic AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B+

data.narrative:
Luma AI is a Series C-stage AI & Software company founded by Amit Jain and Alex Yu, currently valued at $4.0B post-money with $1.07B in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 9 comparable companies—1 public (Palantir) and 8 private firms including Figure AI, Skild AI, Runway, and Magic AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from the Series C post-money of $4.0B, yielding an implied valuation of $6.8B. Peer-Median analysis examines four same-sector, same-stage comparables: Figure AI at $39.0B, Skild AI at $14.0B, Runway at $1.5B, and Magic AI at $1.5B. The median across this cohort is $7.8B, though the wide dispersion ($1.5B to $39.0B) reflects material variance in the AI & Software cohort at Series C.

The two methods yield a triangulated valuation range of $5.8B to $8.9B, with a median of $7.3B. This implies upside from the current $4.0B valuation of approximately 35-80% to the median-to-high range. The primary uncertainty stems from peer-set heterogeneity: the 25.7x spread between minimum and maximum comparable valuations introduces substantial model sensitivity. Additional risks include stage-progression assumptions and the limited public-market data point available for calibration in this sector.
